name: 04-filesystem-prefix
description: Exercises ADR-0005 path qualifier semantics: fs:read with exact path /etc/passwd; fs:write with prefix path /tmp/ (descendants-of semantics).
notes: Per ADR-0005: prefix /tmp/ matches any descendant under /tmp/ (e.g., /tmp/abc, /tmp/nested/dir/file) but NOT /tmp itself. An emitter that wanted to declare read access to /tmp as a directory would emit qualifier path=/tmp (exact), not path=/tmp/ (prefix). POSIX path grammar only; Windows paths deferred to v1.x. Evidence ev-004 (sandbox-syscall open /tmp/abc:WRITE) demonstrates the prefix match: the write was to a descendant of the declared prefix, so no delta.
